[[Category:Aelaki grammar]]

Aelaki adpositions follow the standard four-way morphological pattern (base, reduplication, umlaut, zero-infix), making them highly regular.

== Spatial adpositions ==
{| class="wikitable"
! Base !! Reduplicated !! Umlaut !! Zero (interrogative)
|-
| '''Snü''' "at" || '''Snüsnü''' "through" || '''Snï''' "final location" || '''Snüf''' "where?"
|-
| || '''Snüfsnüf''' "where through?" || || '''Snïf''' "where are they going?"
|}

== Temporal adpositions ==
{| class="wikitable"
! Base !! Reduplicated !! Umlaut !! Zero (interrogative)
|-
| '''Slë''' "at a time" || '''Slëslë''' "throughout a duration" || '''Slæ''' "completion time" || '''Slëf''' "what time?"
|-
| || '''Slëfslë''' "what time period?" || || '''Slæf''' "what time did it end?"
|}

== Morphological pattern ==
The adpositions demonstrate the same four operations that apply across [[Aelaki]] grammar:
* '''Base''': simple location or time
* '''Reduplication''': extended or ongoing (through, throughout)
* '''Umlaut''': completed or final (final location, completion time)
* '''Zero-infix''' (/f/): interrogative (where? when?)

== See also ==
* [[Questions]]
* [[Particles]]

[[Category:git synced pages]]
